title: Render .old-* swap dirs get swept + atomic tmp cleans itself on failure (D6, D2b)
action: Per CONTEXT D6 and D2 (docs/history/tree-hygiene/CONTEXT.md). TWO fixes, both about scratch that leaks on a crash: (A) scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs — cleanStaleTmpDirs (~:155-178) matches ONLY names starting `${base}.tmp-`; extend it to also sweep `${base}.old-*` siblings under the SAME liveness discipline it already uses (never remove a dir whose owning pid is alive), and move the successful-path `rmSync(backupRoot)` (~:208) into the same `finally` that already guards the tmp dir (~:209-211) so a kill between the swap (~:201) and the cleanup cannot leak it. (B) skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/fsutil.mjs writeJsonAtomic (~:87-91) and the jsonl/text atomic variants in templates/lib/decisions.mjs (~:218, ~:983) — wrap the write+rename so a THROW unlinks the tmp before rethrowing (best-effort, never masking the original error). RED-FIRST: write the checks first, run red against unmodified code, record `cells verify --id th-1 --passed false` with the real tail. Checks: a leaked `<base>.old-<deadpid>-x` dir is removed by the next render while a live-pid one is preserved; a forced rename failure (e.g. make the target a directory, or stub renameSync) leaves NO tmp file behind and still throws; existing render race/plugin tests stay green. Mirror fsutil/decisions to .bee/bin byte-identically; render + release_manifest --write + --check. TARGETED verify only (scripts/test_render_race.mjs + the fsutil/misc suite that covers atomic writes) — no full chain (verify ladder D4; the orchestrator runs it at wave close). NOTE: test_render_race scenario (a) is a known WSL2 flake (backlog P2) — rerun once before reporting a failure.

truths: a stale .old-* dir from a dead pid is swept by the next render; a live-pid one is never touched
the .old-* removal is in the finally path, not only the success path
a failed atomic rename leaves no .tmp file and still throws the original error
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never remove a sibling dir owned by a LIVE pid (concurrent render safety)
Never swallow the original error when cleaning up a failed write
Do not move the atomic tmp out of the target's directory (EXDEV/atomicity — D2)

trace.dropped_reason: re-scoped: user clarified the real problem is forgotten scratch/report files with no canonical home + no sweep, not .bak backups
